items.title: Calls grow to strengthen child protection laws after Selayang abuse claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Calls are mounting for the government to strengthen key child protection laws following disturbing allegations of abuse at an unregistered care home in Selayang, with legal experts warning that weak enforcement has left vulnerable children exposed. Former Malaysian Bar president Salim Bashir said urgent amendments are needed to existing legislation, including the Child Act 2001 and the Child Care Centre Act 1984, to ensure stricter enforcement and tougher penalties against operators of unregistered childcare facilities. "The government should take meaningful action by amending and strengthening legislation concerning children, which would provide a stricter approach and harsher penalties in cases involving unregistered care homes," he told the New Straits Times.

items.title: Honda Malaysia vendors back ministry's development-driven policy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Honda Malaysia Vendors Club (HMVC) has expressed support for the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry's development-driven investment approach, saying it is crucial for sustaining growth and strengthening the country's automotive ecosystem. HMVC president Kam Cheong Loong said close collaboration between the government, private sector and multinational companies has long been a key factor in the evolution of Malaysia's automotive industry. He said the ministry's handling of proposed inward investments, including those involving China-based electric vehicle (EV) maker BYD, reflects a calibrated approach that balances foreign investment with national priorities, particularly in the emerging EV space.

items.title: Anwar credits federal policies for Johor growth, highlights Forest City revival
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said federal government policies and support had laid the foundation for Johor's strong economic growth. "Johor has indeed recorded good economic growth, but much of it is due to the federal government's vision. Forest City, near Johor Baru, faces slow sales and under‑occupation, prompting government efforts to revive the development.

items.title: SMKPK Bumbong Lima teachers' dedication leads to excellent SPM 2025 results
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: The unwavering dedication of teachers at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Pendidikan Khas (SMKPK) Bumbong Lima, in nurturing students with special needs, particularly those with autism, has borne fruit, with the school's 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) candidates achieving excellent results. School principal Rosiah Mohamad Ariff said the success reflects the collective efforts of teachers who never gave up in guiding students with special educational needs (MBPK) through learning and examination challenges. They understand the emotional and behavioural needs of autistic students, who require different approaches.

items.title: OPEC+ debates theoretical oil output hike amid Iran war paralysis, sources say
items.summary: MOSCOW/LONDON: OPEC+ may approve an oil output increase on Sunday, four sources from the group said, a rise that will largely exist on paper as its key members are unable to raise production due to the US-Israeli war with Iran. Other group members such as Russia are unable to increase output due to Western sanctions and damage to infrastructure inflicted during the war with Ukraine. Several Gulf officials have said it would take months to resume normal operations and reach production targets even if the war stopped and Hormuz reopened immediately.

items.title: Cheri and Rambai help relocate wild elephant, bringing relief to 500 villagers
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: Two decoy elephants, Cheri and Rambai, were deployed during an operation at Felda Aring 10 here to help coax a wild male elephant to be moved to a more suitable habitat. Khaidir Ibrahim, 39, said the wild elephant conflict had persisted for nearly two years, causing constant fear among the community. "Alhamdullilah, Perhilitan has finally managed to capture two wild bulls that were roaming Felda Aring 10 and 11.

items.title: Police hunt driver over viral wrong-way incident on Jalan Klang Lama
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are tracking down a driver seen travelling against traffic along Jalan Klang Lama in a video that has gone viral on social media. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said authorities were alerted to the incident at 11.38am and immediately launched checks. "The incident is believed to have occurred at about 9.44am on April 5 along Jalan Klang Lama, from the Jalan Klang Lama–Jalan Sepadu intersection to the Jalan Klang Lama–Jalan Kuchai Lama intersection, covering an estimated distance of about 500 metres," he said in a statement.

items.title: JPJ audits trailer company after Segamat crash kills three, driver found positive for meth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has launched a safety audit on the trailer company involved in a crash that killed three people in Segamat, as authorities move to tighten scrutiny on operator compliance. The audit, carried out under the department’s “JPJ Inspection and Safety Audit” (JISA), will examine whether the company met required safety standards following the fatal collision at Kilometre 212 of the Johor Bahru–Seremban road on April 2. “In line with instructions from Transport Minister Anthony Loke, JPJ is conducting a JISA audit on the trailer operator to ensure all safety aspects are adhered to, based on requirements set by APAD for the company.

items.title: Thousands gather outside US embassy in Jakarta to mourn fallen Indonesian peackeepers
items.summary: JAKARTA: Thousands of people gathered outside the United States Embassy in central Jakarta today to express condolences for the three Indonesian military personnel killed while serving under the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL). The three personnel have been identified as Major Inf (Posthumous) Zulmi Aditya Iskandar, Sergeant Major (Posthumous) M. Nur Ichwan, and Corporal First Class (Posthumous) Farizal Rhomadhon. Five other TNI personnel were injured in the March 29 and 30 incidents, namely Private First Class Rico Pramudia who sustained serious injuries, while four others, First Lieutenant (Inf) Sulthan Wirdean Maulana, Private First Class Deni Rianto, Private First Class Bayu Prakoso, and Private First Class Arif Kurniawan suffered minor injuries.

items.title: Anwar: PKR machinery must bridge government, public gap
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on Johor PKR to strengthen unity at all levels and ensure the party machinery serves the people effectively. Speaking at the Johor PKR convention today, Anwar emphasised the importance of maintaining a mature and disciplined party that remains committed to its reformist mandate since its inception. "Johor PKR must continue to strengthen the spirit of togetherness at all levels, regardless of rank.

items.title: No free passes: Anwar tells PKR leaders to earn GE16 spots through groundwork
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned PKR leaders there will be no automatic tickets to contest in the next general election, urging party divisions — especially in Johor — to prove their strength on the ground or risk being overlooked. Speaking at the Johor Keadilan Convention 2026 in Johor Bahru, the PKR president said positions within the party — whether division, Women’s or Youth chief — do not guarantee candidacy, according to The New Straits Times . “A division chief is not an automatic candidate, nor is a Women or Youth chief,” he said, stressing that only those who work, show dedication and are capable of winning would be considered.

items.title: 3 Bangladeshis, Thai national jailed for overstaying and illegal entry
items.summary: PASIR MAS: Three Bangladeshis and a Thai national were imprisoned after failing to pay fines for overstaying and illegal entry. The three Bangladeshis, aged 25, 28 and 38, were charged under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act for overstaying. The Thai national, aged 49, was charged under Section 6(1)(c) of the same Act for illegal entry.. A court official on condition of anonymity told the New Straits Times that the 4 men failed to pay the fine.

items.title: Sabah Umno respects Abdul Rahman Dahlan decision to resign as division chief
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah Umno respects Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Dahlan's decision to resign as the party's Tuaran division chief, said state liaison chief Datuk Jafry Ariffin. It was reported that Abdul Rahman, who was the state deputy chief, had announced his resignation as the Tuaran Umno chief. In a statement yesterday, Abdul Rahman said his resignation was based on personal considerations and not related to any issues within the party.

items.title: Farmers urged to switch to organic fertilisers amid El Nino concerns
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Farmers are encouraged to switch to organic fertilisers to reduce reliance on imported agricultural inputs and lower costs. Sabah Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said the prolonged El Nino weather phenomenon is expected to disrupt fertiliser supply, potentially driving up production costs. The Melalap assemblyman said the Sabah Agriculture Department has implemented various training programmes to help farmers produce their own fertiliser using plant and food waste.

items.title: Incentives for carmaker must drive supply chain growth, says PVA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Incentives for investments in the national automotive industry should create a multiplier effect, driving growth across the entire supply chain and supporting vendor development, said the Proton Vendors Association (PVA). Hence, the PVA said it fully supported the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry (MITI) in its efforts to safeguard and strengthen Malaysia's automotive ecosystem. The PVA said incentives channelled to carmakers should also support the development of Tier 1, Tier 2 and Tier 3 vendors.

items.title: Skip outdoor play: Nancy advises childcare centres to shield kids from heat
items.summary: KUCHING, April 5 — Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri has urged all childcare centres to suspend outdoor activities in hot weather to ensure the well-being of children, particularly those under three. “Operators are reminded to keep children in protected areas. Also present was Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof.

items.title: Muhyiddin: PM should not call critics 'ignorant', must avoid derogatory remarks on energy crisis
items.summary: MUAR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has reminded Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to maintain decorum and avoid using derogatory labels against those criticising the government's handling of the energy crisis. PN deputy chairman Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said the prime minister should remain open to feedback instead of labelling critics ignorant following backlash over rising fuel costs linked to the Middle East conflict. Muhyiddin said that as the prime minister, Anwar should view such opinions as a challenge to improve rather than a personal affront.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia's diplomatic efforts on Iran, Gaza issues should be given credit
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Credit should be given to the government's efforts to strengthen diplomatic relations and take a firm stance on issues involving Iran and the Gaza conflict, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said the government's firm stance, including its protests against Israeli and United States attacks on Iran, had helped bolster Malaysia's diplomatic relations, allowing Malaysian ships to pass safely through the Strait of Hormuz. he said.

items.title: National Harmony Act needed to bridge communities, says Lee Lam Thye
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: It is time for Malaysia to introduce a National Harmony Act to bridge communities, foster mutual respect and safeguard the unity that has long defined the nation. Lee said the proposed National Harmony Act would serve several key purposes, including providing a clear legal framework to promote respect, tolerance and peaceful coexistence while addressing acts that incite hatred without curbing legitimate expression. He said the National Harmony Act should complement existing laws, not replace them, and must be carefully crafted to protect harmony without restricting legitimate democratic expression.

items.title: Three days on, body of missing wheelchair-bound man recovered from Kuala Selangor river
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 5 — An elderly man in a wheelchair was found drowned in Sungai Kampung Kuantan, Kuala Selangor, today, three days after he was reported missing after falling into the river. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) deputy director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the body of the 63-year-old man was discovered at about 8.30am by a member of the public near the Batu Tujuh Setengah jetty. The incident was reported at 9.09pm on April 3,” he said in a statement.

items.title: AI boom lifts Frontken outlook: Kenanga Research
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Frontken Corp Bhd should benefit from the ongoing artificial intelligence (AI) upcycle, underpinned by rising semiconductor demand and strong foundry utilisation, Kenanga Research said. Kenanga said Frontken's specialised equipment cleaning expertise and long-standing relationships with leading global foundries position it well to capitalise on the AI-driven capital expenditure cycle. Kenanga Research noted that higher semiconductor equipment cleaning volumes will be driven by increased fab utilisation, particularly at Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC), where utilisation is expected to remain high at around 95 per cent, up from 90 per cent previously.

items.title: Multi-agency effort under Clean Rice initiative delivers strong harvest results
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A pioneering government-industry collaboration to revitalise Malaysia's padi sector is showing clear, measurable success, with the Clean Rice initiative emerging as a scalable model to boost yields, restore farmer confidence and strengthen national food security. The breakthrough was showcased at the First Clean Rice Walkabout in Kampung Permatang Rotan, Penanti here yesterday, launched by Agriculture Department director-general Datuk Nor Sam Alwi. Driven by ABS Greentech Sdn Bhd in partnership with Humate USA Inc, and supported by the Penang Agriculture Department, the initiative underscores how innovation and coordinated execution can transform a traditionally challenged sector.

items.title: No more stage bus route cancellations, says Perak rep after commuter complaints
items.summary: IPOH, April 5 — No more stage bus routes in Perak will be discontinued, including the Kuala Kangsar-Lenggong-Gerik route, following concerns among commuters, Perak Infrastructure, Energy, Water and Public Transport Committee chairman Datuk Mohammad Nizar Jamaluddin said. “We understand the concerns of users, especially students who depend on this bus service for school. Mohammad Nizar said that to ensure the continuity of public transport services, the state government is also planning to introduce new routes covering the Kampar and Gopeng areas.

items.title: Authorities bust subsidised diesel syndicate, seize over RM320k in goods
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: Authorities have crippled a syndicate involved in the misappropriation of subsidised diesel, seizing nearly RM328,000 worth of goods in a raid here last night. Penang Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) enforcement officers who moved in on the premises found evidence of ongoing illegal activities involving controlled subsidised goods. State KPDN director S. Jegan said the modus operandi involved the illegal transfer of subsidised diesel between tanker lorries at an unauthorised storage site.

items.title: MACC: Compliance alone insufficient to sustain integrity reforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia must move beyond strengthening compliance systems and focus on building genuine trust and an ethical culture to sustain integrity reforms. He said integrity initiatives across organisations are typically built on awareness, enforcement and monitoring, including ethics training, policy enforcement, internal audits and reporting systems aligned with global standards. Azlan said inconsistencies remain a major issue, with programmes frequently affected by limited resources, competing priorities and operational pressures.

items.title: Bukit Merah at critical stage as Perak keeps close watch on dam levels amid prolonged heat
items.summary: State Infrastructure, Energy, Water and Transportation Committee chairman Datuk Seri Mohammad Nizar Jamaluddin said the Bukit Merah dam’s water level stands at 6.965m, continuing to decline and now at Stage One Critical Irrigation status. He said that the Temengor dam has experienced a continuous decline in water levels since early January, with the current level at 239.07m, approaching the dam’s safe operational critical level of 238m. “Overall, dams at critical levels require continuous monitoring, disciplined management of water releases, and prudent water usage,” he told a press conference here today.

items.title: Keadilan convention: Anwar tells party leaders posts are no guarantee of GE candidacy
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: PKR president Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded division leaders, particularly in Johor, to intensify efforts in building party strength ahead of the next general election. He said that holding a position as a division, Women, or Youth chief was no guarantee of being selected as a candidate in the next general election. Therefore, you are reminded to mobilise the party's strength."

items.title: Wrong way on Old Klang Road: KL police seek white Proton Saga driver after viral video
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Police are searching for the driver of a Proton Saga after a video showing the car travelling against traffic along Jalan Klang Lama went viral. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said police were alerted to the 40-second clip at about 11.38am today. Initial checks found the incident is believed to have taken place at 9.44am, along a roughly 500m stretch between the Jalan Klang Lama/Jalan Sepadu junction and the Jalan Klang Lama/Jalan Kuchai Lama junction.

items.title: Middle East war hits Britain's fish and chip shops
items.summary: BEFORE war erupted in the Middle East, fisherman Peter Bruce spent about £5,000 (US$6,600) on diesel to trawl the North Sea for haddock and cod, the main species used in fish and chips. It was not yet clear whether the rise in energy prices would have an immediate effect on the price of fish, Bruce said. The classic recipe for fish and chips – white fish deep-fried in batter, chips, and mushy peas, often with tartare sauce on the side – dates back to the 1860s.

items.title: No polls, no distractions: Umno backs Anwar, says tackle cost-of-living crisis first
items.summary: MANJUNG, April 5 — Umno has welcomed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s indication that the 16th general election (GE16) will not be held in the near future, asserting instead that priority must be given to addressing the rising cost of living. Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said that, given current global uncertainties, all parties should set aside power struggles and focus on the welfare of the people. Also present was Umno Supreme Council (MKT) member Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir, who is also the Lumut Umno Division Chief.

items.title: PN ready for talks with govt on energy crisis, says Muhyiddin
items.summary: MUAR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) is ready to sit down with the Madani government to find solutions as the world grapples with a global energy crisis. PN deputy chairman Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said the opposition welcomed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's call for cooperation but said that the invitation must be sincere. "The prime minister must be sincere in this effort to resolve issues arising from the Middle East crisis.

items.title: Anwar defends strong Iran position, says Malaysia’s stance delivered results
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed the hope that credit should be given to the government’s efforts to strengthen diplomatic relations and voice a firm stance on issues involving Iran and the Gaza conflict instead of being disputed by certain parties. He said this was because the government’s firm stance, including its stance in protesting against Israeli and United States attacks on Iran, had helped strengthen the country’s diplomatic relations, thus allowing Malaysian ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. Also present were Communications Minister, Datuk Fahmi Fadzil who is also PKR information chief, PKR vice president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan and Johor PKR State Leadership Council (MPN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa.

items.title: Man arrested over security guard's death in Klang
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have arrested a man suspected of killing a security guard with a car steering lock during an altercation in Klang early this morning. Klang Utara police chief S. Vijaya Rao said the incident occurred about 1am at a security post in a residential area in Bandar Bukit Raja. Vijaya said officers from the Klang Utara district police headquarters (IPD) later tracked down and arrested the suspect, and seized a car steering lock believed to have been used in the incident.

items.title: Terengganu MB calls for stronger Muslim unity amid geopolitical strain at ummah meet
items.summary: KUALA NERUS: Muslim unity must be continuously strengthened as a bulwark against present-day challenges, particularly geopolitical strain and an increasingly turbulent world, Terengganu Menteri Besar Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said. The programme's flagship event was also attended by Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang, Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud, Bersatu vice-president Datuk Dr Mohd Radzi Md Jidin, and Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. Other leaders present included Pejuang president Datuk Seri Mukhriz Mahathir, Berjasa president Zamani Ibrahim, Pas secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, Gabungan Nasionalis chairman Aminuddin Yahaya, and Muafakat Nasional president Tan Sri Annuar Musa.

items.title: Muhyiddin says Opposition open to energy crisis talks, warns Putrajaya against tokenism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Perikatan Nasional (PN) has said it is ready to work with the government to tackle the global energy crisis, but warned against what it described as token gestures of cooperation. According to Berita Harian , its deputy chairman, Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, welcomed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s call for bipartisan engagement, but urged that any invitation be made in earnest. Muhyiddin, however, said leadership of the crisis response should rest with the prime minister, arguing that the issue went beyond fuel prices to broader public hardship.

items.title: Govt to speed up diesel subsidy reimbursements for petrol station operators
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry will engage the Finance Ministry to expedite reimbursement payments to petrol station operators facing cash flow pressures under the targeted diesel subsidy scheme. Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the ministry acknowledged concerns raised by operators, who claimed they were required to front capital for diesel purchases at elevated prices. Media reports yesterday said petrol station operators claimed they had effectively become unofficial financiers of the targeted diesel subsidy distribution due to the upfront payment structure.

items.title: Body of 63-year-old wheelchair-bound man found 5.5km from where he fell into river
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said the victim, identified as Ravi a/l Periasamy, was discovered by members of the public about 8.30am near a jetty at Mile 7½. "Firefighters were sent to retrieve the body," he said when contacted today. Mukhlis said a search and rescue operation was launched immediately using water surface search techniques, with assistance from the Water Rescue Team (PPDA) from the Shah Alam and Port Klang fire stations, as well as the K9 unit.

items.title: Nik Nazmi to PKR: Do not 'lose your way' at the height of power
items.summary: The reminder came from its former vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, who recalled the party's early days when it was driven by hope and idealism as a platform to champion the reform agenda and multiracial politics. The Setiawangsa member of parliament also questioned whether the party is now more inclined to find excuses to delay change rather than working to implement the reform agenda it has long championed. "Now, PKR is not only a government party, but also a PM (prime minister) party.

items.title: Structural reforms needed to tackle food security risks
items.summary: LETTER: Global food systems are entering a dangerous phase, driven by the combined pressures of climate change and geopolitical conflict. In conflict zones, food systems have collapsed — with production disrupted, supply chains severed and prices soaring. Climate-induced droughts and extreme weather are further weakening global food output.

items.title: Easter brings faithful together across Kuching in celebration and prayer
items.summary: KUCHING, April 5 — A massive number of Christian congregants gathered at their cathedrals on Easter Sunday to celebrate the resurrection of Jesus Christ, in what clergy described as the foundation of the Christian faith. Following the 7am English-language service, a Mandarin service was held at 9am, followed by Bahasa Malaysia service at 11am. The Bahasa Malaysia service took place at 10.30am, the Mandarin service at 11am, and the Iban service was held at 2pm.

items.title: Kayaking incident: MOE to assist families of two trainee teachers who drowned
items.summary: KANGAR: The Education Ministry (MOE) is providing immediate assistance to the families of two trainee teachers who drowned during a kayaking activity in Kedah. In a statement, the ministry said psychosocial support is also being offered, with attention given to the welfare of those affected. "MOE expresses its deepest condolences to the families of the two Perlis campus Institute of Teacher Education (IPG) students involved in the incident during an off-campus outdoor education programme on Saturday.

items.title: Education Ministry offers support, launches probe after two trainee teachers drown at Pantai Merdeka in Kedah
items.summary: KANGAR, April 5 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) is arranging immediate assistance to the families of two students from the Perlis campus of the Institute of Teacher Education (IPG), who drowned in a kayaking incident at Pantai Merdeka near Sungai Petani, Kedah, yesterday. “MOE expresses its deepest condolences to the families of the two Perlis campus IPG students involved in the incident during an off-campus outdoor education programme on Saturday. Meanwhile, based on the official Facebook page of the IPG campus, the two students were identified as Christopher Ling Jia Siang, 21, and Joey Ling Lin Siang, 20.

items.title: Anwar steps in with aid for families after seven homes lost in Menggatal blaze
items.summary: The assistance was presented by Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud. “Today, I, along with officers from the Prime Minister’s Department’s Implementation Coordination Unit, represented the prime minister to present contributions to the victims of the fire incident. The prime minister also conveyed his condolences to all the victims involved,” he said.

items.title: Kedah housewife faces court over RM173,500 ‘phantom jewellery’ scam, pleads not guilty
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 5 — A housewife pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to eight counts of cheating over orders for non-existent jewellery worth RM173,500 two years ago. Siti Hajartul Haini Abd Hamid, 39, entered the plea after all eight charges against her were read out before Judge Sharmila Abdul Samad. According to the charges, she is accused of defrauding a 55-year-old woman by deceiving the victim into depositing money into her bank account for non-existent jewellery in Kampung Mani Mukim Belimbing Kanan, Padang Terap, from June 2 to August 16, 2024.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues storm warning across Negeri Sembilan, Pahang, Sarawak and Sabah until 4pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds affecting five states until 4pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the affected areas in Pahang are Bentong and Rompin, while in Negeri Sembilan, they include Jelebu and Kuala Pilah. A similar weather warning has also been issued for Sarawak, involving Kuching (Bau and Kuching), Serian (Serian), Samarahan (Samarahan and Simunjan), Sri Aman (Sri Aman), Sarikei (Julau and Meradong), Sibu, Mukah, Kapit (Song, Kapit and Bukit Mabong) and Bintulu (Tatau).

items.title: Air quality breaches unhealthy levels in four areas, Putrajaya hovers near 150
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Air quality deteriorated across parts of the Klang Valley and Negeri Sembilan today, with four areas breaching unhealthy levels and Putrajaya emerging as the hardest hit, even as broader national data suggests conditions remain mixed. Data from the Department of Environment’s Air Pollution Index Management System (APIMS) showed Putrajaya, Jenjarom in Selangor, and Seremban and Nilai in Negeri Sembilan all recorded Air Pollutant Index (API) readings above 100 as of 10am. Nationwide, most monitoring stations remained within safer ranges, with 15 stations recording good air quality and 48 at moderate levels.

items.title: Cost of diesel subsidies have tripled to RM2.2b from RM700m, says domestic trade minister
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 5 — The monthly allocation for the Subsidised Diesel Control Scheme (SKDS) is estimated to have risen to RM2.2 billion in March, nearly three times higher than about RM700 million previously, following a sharp increase in global diesel prices. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the surge in expenditure was driven by escalating global diesel prices. "Under the SKDS, a total of 33 types of vehicles are eligible for subsidised diesel, comprising 10 categories of public land transport vehicles and 23 categories of goods transport vehicles.

items.title: Man arrested after security guard beaten to death in Klang housing estate, car lock seized
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — A security guard has died after being assaulted at a residential estate in Bandar Bukit Raja, Klang, with police arresting a man believed to be responsible hours later. North Klang district police chief Assistant Commissioner S. Vijaya Rao said the attack was reported at about 1am at a guardhouse in the housing area today. Police later tracked down the suspect and seized a car lock believed to have been used in the assault.

items.title: Dead on paper, alive in KL: Man’s 17-year fight to prove he’s no ghost in the system
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — For nearly two decades, Mohd Rosli Idris has lived a life few can imagine — alive in person, but officially dead on paper. Rosli, who was then working as a second-hand clothing seller around Chow Kit, said the mix-up began when his former employer encountered a gravely ill man who closely resembled him. With no identification on the man, the employer contacted Rosli’s cousin.

items.title: Mount Kinabalu’s Unesco World Heritage status not up for trade in cable car debate, says Sabah DCM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — There can be no compromise on the conditions underpinning Mount Kinabalu’s Unesco World Heritage status, Sabah’s deputy chief minister has said, as a proposed cable car project remains under study. Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun said any development around the mountain must prioritise environmental preservation and its heritage value, warning against viewing the proposal purely through a commercial lens. However, we must not forget that Mount Kinabalu is a World Heritage Site.

items.title: Injured man among two stranded hikers rescued after overnight operation in Sarawak national park
items.summary: SIBU, April 5 — The Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) search and rescue team rescued two hikers who were stranded at Lambir Hills National Park early this morning. “The two hikers rescued early this morning were identified as Gordon Brian, 32, who sustained a leg injury, and Wan Mohamad Hidayat, 42, who was unhurt,” the statement read. The operations centre had earlier received an emergency call at 6.30pm yesterday reporting that three hikers were stranded in the national park.

items.title: Police arrest suspect in Kajang MRT station sexual harassment case after viral clip
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — A man suspected of committing an act of sexual harassment at Kajang MRT station, in an incident that went viral on the Threads app, has been arrested, police said yesterday. Kajang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the 24-year-old man was detained at his home in Bandar Baru Bangi, Kajang. Earlier, media reports said police were tracking down a man believed to have committed an act of sexual harassment at the Kajang MRT station exit, following the circulation of the video on Threads.

items.title: Don’t cut frontline fuel: Ex-minister moots 300-litre subsidy carve-out for healthcare workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — A targeted petrol subsidy of up to 300 litres for healthcare workers should be considered, particularly those carrying out regular field duties, Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa has said. The Backbenchers Club chairman said some public healthcare staff rely on their own vehicles to visit chronic patients, monitor infectious diseases and carry out community health interventions. However, she said a targeted approach could ensure healthcare workers with high mobility needs continue to receive support without undermining subsidy rationalisation.

items.title: From Sabah to Sarawak: St John Ambulans Malaysia pulls off complex 600km emergency transfer
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 5 — St John Ambulans Malaysia (SJAM) Sabah completed a medical transfer covering more than 600km from here to Bintulu, Sarawak, on April 1. State Superintendent Jansen Sai told Daily Express that the operation underscored the organisation’s capability in executing long-distance emergency medical services. He said the mission was led by State Commander Ng Ming Syoung, with support from First Aider Joe and State Staff Nursing Officer Janet.

items.title: Hamzah teases shift without moving: Opposition Leader hints at PAS-linked ‘new home’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Opposition Leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin has deepened the intrigue over his political future, suggesting his much-talked-about “new home” may not be new at all, but an existing party already within the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition, according to Sinar Harian . “When we say a new home, does it mean a new party or an existing party? “Yes… it could be an existing party.

items.title: Search teams recover second boy’s body after Jerantut river tragedy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — The body of 14-year-old Muhammad Amirul Ashraf Abdullah, a student of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Jengka 24, was found by search and rescue (SAR) teams in Jerantut at about 9.50am today. According to Berita Harian , Jerantut fire station chief Raja Arif Raja Shamsudin said the body was recovered and brought ashore by rescuers. The body of a second victim, Muhammad Danial Haikal Shamsudin, had been found earlier at about 4.20pm yesterday, roughly 2km from the same location.

items.title: Kedah’s Kota Setar swelters under Level 2 heatwave as temperatures hit 40°C
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — Parts of northern Malaysia are baking under escalating heat conditions, with Kota Setar in Kedah continuing to record Level 2 heatwave status as temperatures climb as high as 40°C. These include Perlis and several districts across Kedah — Kuala Muda, Kubang Pasu, Pokok Sena, Pendang, Padang Terap, Sik and Baling — signalling widespread heat stress across the northern peninsula. Similar alert-level conditions have also been recorded in parts of Perak, Kelantan, Pahang and Johor, including Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kuala Krai, Pasir Mas, Tanah Merah, Machang, Maran, Jerantut and Segamat.

items.title: Motorcyclist dies in Kota Bharu pile-up after speeding car veers off road, hits four parked vehicles
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — A 41-year-old motorcyclist was killed after a car lost control and ploughed into him before crashing into four parked vehicles in a six-vehicle pile-up in Kota Bharu early today. Kota Bharu district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Azmir Damiri said officers received a report at 1.10am. “Upon reaching the scene, the car veered onto the left shoulder towards Kubang Kerian and struck a motorcyclist who was stopped by the roadside.

items.title: Bomba expands grassroots fire response with 500 volunteer brigade target by 2030
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 5 — The Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) is optimistic about achieving its target of establishing 500 Volunteer Fire Brigade (PBS) teams nationwide by 2030, in line with its fire services policy and strategic plan. Director-general Datuk Nor Hisham Mohammad said the target is considered realistic based on current momentum, as well as continued commitment from various parties, including state governments and local communities. He was met after the closing ceremony of the PBS Convention and the Penang JBPM Penang Public Safety and Awareness Campaign, officiated by Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, at the Batu Kawan Stadium last night.

items.title: Aaron urges Malaysians to uphold unity and compassion in Easter message
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 5 — National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang has called on Malaysians to continue fostering interfaith harmony with mutual respect and compassion to ensure the nation’s unity remains intact. In a Facebook post in conjunction with Easter, he said Malaysia is built on diversity and that peace and unity must always remain central among the people. “Happy Easter!
